title: Elderly Glioblastoma: Long-Course Versus Short-Course Chemoradiotherapy: A Regional New Zealand Retrospective Review.
abstract: Elderly patients make up a large proportion of those diagnosed with glioblastoma. There are multiple treatment options available for elderly patients including conventional and hypofractionated chemoradiotherapy, chemotherapy monotherapy and best supportive care; therefore, management decision-making can be complex. This retrospective study aims to compare overall survival (OS) and review prognostic factors in patients aged 65 and above who received either long-course or short-course chemoradiotherapy in regional New Zealand.
